canonical_definition: The task of generating a fluent textual translation in a spoken language from a given sign language video sequence.
papers: Uni-Sign: Toward Unified Sign Language Understanding at Scale
YouTube-SL-25: A Large-Scale, Open-Domain Multilingual Sign Language Parallel Corpus

definitions.text: The task of generating a fluent textual translation in a spoken language from a given sign language video sequence.
definitions.paper: Uni-Sign: Toward Unified Sign Language Understanding at Scale

definitions.text: The task of generating written text that corresponds to the meaning of a sequence of signs in a video.

relationships.evidence: SLT task is conducted on the CSL-Daily, How2Sign (Duarte et al., 2021), and OpenASL (Shi et al., 2022) datasets. (Section 4.2)

relationships.paper: YouTube-SL-25: A Large-Scale, Open-Domain Multilingual Sign Language Parallel Corpus
relationships.evidence: We see that on both translation and lang id, sign language pretraining is substantially better than none (as in Uthus et al. [35]) and multilingual transfer helps both the higher-resource sign language (ASL) and the lower-resource sign languages within YouTube-SL-25.
